description: A devastating oomycete disease affecting peppers, squash, and other cucurbits that causes rapid wilting, dark stem lesions, and white moldy fruit rot. It thrives in warm wet conditions and poorly drained soil. The pathogen produces swimming zoospores that spread through saturated soil and splashing water.

life_cycle: Oospores survive in soil for years. In warm wet conditions, they produce sporangia that release swimming zoospores. Zoospores spread through saturated soil and splashing water to infect stems, roots, and fruit in contact with soil. Disease spreads rapidly during heavy rain.

Use raised beds in heavy soil
Avoid overhead irrigation

description: A caterpillar pest that feeds on flower buds and blooms of petunias, geraniums, nicotiana, and other garden flowers. It bores into buds before they open, destroying them from within. Damage often goes unnoticed until plants fail to bloom. Also damages tobacco, cotton, and some vegetable crops.

life_cycle: Moths lay eggs on flower buds and foliage. Larvae bore into buds to feed. After 2-3 weeks, larvae drop to soil to pupate. Multiple generations per year in warm climates. Adults are active at dusk.
